achievements: Removed ASYNCIFY dependency — switched to global GameState struct pattern, enabling emscripten_set_main_loop without blocking/coroutine overhead
Increased card capacity to CARD_COUNT=2000 with a sliding texture window (WINDOW_HALF=8) for memory-efficient streaming
Added gamepad support (D-pad) alongside keyboard arrow navigation
Implemented key-repeat with configurable REPEAT_DELAY/REPEAT_RATE for held-key scrolling
Improved CI build pipeline for GitHub Pages deployment
Reduced demo poster.png size to improve initial load time on web
improvements: CardFilename() is a stub — it always returns 'poster.png'. The per-card filename logic is commented out with a TODO, meaning the carousel is non-functional for real data
WINDOW_HALF=8 loads 17 textures at a time — fine for desktop, but on web with potentially large card images this could spike memory; consider making it configurable or smaller for web builds
No touch/swipe input — only keyboard and gamepad are supported, limiting mobile web usability
The help text ('Navigate with D-Pad / Arrows') only shows at card 0 and disappears permanently — consider a fade-out timer instead
Matrix transform chain rebuilds MatrixIdentity() every frame for all CARD_COUNT cards — pre-computing visible range only would reduce CPU work
The EMSCRIPTEN preload embeds only poster.png; a real deployment would need a different asset strategy for 2000 unique cards
next_tasks: Implement real CardFilename() logic — accept a data source (file list, JSON manifest, or directory scan) and wire up per-card images
Add touch/swipe gesture support via raylib's gesture API (GetGestureDetected, GESTURE_SWIPE_LEFT/RIGHT) for mobile web
Add a card index/counter HUD (e.g. '42 / 2000') so users know their position in a large collection
Support loading a card list from a JSON or CSV file embedded in the web build, so the demo is self-contained and useful
Add mouse wheel/trackpad scroll navigation as a third input method
Consider adding a search/jump-to feature for large collections (keyboard shortcut to enter a number)
risks: Web memory ceiling: 2000 cards × any real texture size can exhaust the 64MB INITIAL_MEMORY even with the window strategy — ALLOW_MEMORY_GROWTH is set but wasm heap growth is slow and can fail on iOS Safari

Single-file emscripten build (-sSINGLE_FILE=1) will be very large once real card images are preloaded; the current demo only embeds one poster.png, but a real dataset would require a streaming/fetch strategy instead of --preload-file
No error handling when LoadTexture fails — the code falls back to placeholder silently, but errors won't surface to the user or a log
CMakeLists.txt references 'vmlib' in the WIN32 block (line 41) which doesn't exist in this project — this is a latent bug that will break Windows native builds
raylib is pinned to 6.0 via zip URL — no version lock file, so a re-download could break if the upstream tag moves or is deleted
